slides.kicker: // sample template · maritime shipping and ports
slides.lead: Demo presentation of the template. All text is sample (dummy) content and only exists to show off the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. This is where the event name, a one-line subtitle and the bottom meta row belong. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, five views on the same harbor.
slides.notes: Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a colored circle, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions in between.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Terminal throughput, vessel data and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.h1: The data is already at the dock.
slides.lead: AIS feeds, crane sensors and terminal systems collect more than anyone can read. Value appears only when they connect.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, a striking illustration, a short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: fewer vessel waiting hours after smart berth scheduling
saved daily on manual yard reporting
higher crane moves per hour after optimization
bullets: Measure impact, not activity: what matters is the effect on the quay.
Start where you already have data and where the pain is sharpest.
A small pilot with a clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a caption, supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The model does not wait for congestion, it forecasts it.
bullets: Vessel ETAs, tide windows and crane availability.
What preceded past berth conflicts and delays.
Before the queue builds at the harbor mouth.
Berth slots into the window with least idle time.
slides.footnote: // two column slide with an illustration
slides.notes: Two column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself up.

slides.lead: Same terminal, two approaches and two cost curves.
cards: We react once vessels are already stacked up. Costly and unpredictable.
Berths by a fixed timetable. Better, but often idle too early.
Berths by real demand and tides. Lowest total cost.
Critical liners predictive, the rest scheduled.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing options.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a smart port.
cards: Crane and gate data in real time, close to the source.
Live views of terminal flow and deviations.
Flags unusual vessel or yard behavior before staff do.
From data straight to a daily brief for leadership.
Data stays inside your network and under control.
A crew that understands the tools and trusts them.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Growth slows, so it pays to finish the basics first.
Trade volumes rise fast and reshape established routines.
Automation starts to plan moves, not just compute them.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: It is not the port with the most data that wins, but the one that turns it into a decision the fastest.
slides.by: Sample quote to show the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

bullets: Every tool has the name of a person who stands behind it.
You know in advance how you will tell it worked.
On critical moves a person always makes the call.
slides.notes: A plain slide with bullets and a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the area.

slides.lead: A pilot with no metric and no owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.h1: A report from data in 30 seconds.
slides.lead: We switch into the tool and build a daily terminal brief live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
